channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Bands drop out of Orlando music festival after Kyle Rittenhouse booked as guest
item.description: Shell Shock organizers describe the festival as a concert charity event that seeks to raise funds for a charity that "supports first responders struggling with injuries to include Post Traumatic Stress Disorder (PTSD).
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, Fox 5

item.title: The Latest: Harris to visit Michigan while Trump heads to Georgia
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris will visit the union stronghold of Flint, Michigan, on Friday as she battles with Donald Trump for working-class voters who could tip the scales in this year’s election. Her appearance in the battleground state comes the day after U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge denies an order sought by a Black student who was punished over his hair
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A federal judge on Friday denied a request by a Black high school student in Texas for a court order that the student’s lawyers say would have allowed him to return to his high school without fear of having his previous punishment over his hairstyle resume.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What renters should know about breaking a lease to buy a home
item.description: Renters can break their lease to purchase their own home but they should understand the ramifications of leaving early
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, Fox 5

item.title: Man charged with helping Idaho inmate escape during a hospital ambush sentenced to life in prison
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — An Idaho man who prosecutors said ambushed and shot correctional officers at a Boise hospital in a plot to help a fellow white supremacist gang member escape from prison was sentenced to life behind bars on Friday.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ex- Virginia cop who killed shoplifting suspect acquitted of manslaughter, guilty on firearm charge
item.description: A jury on Friday acquitted a former police officer in Virginia of involuntary manslaughter after he fatally shot a shoplifting suspect last year outside a busy shopping mall.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Barakat And Olivia Diaz, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Some perplexed at jury's mixed verdict in trial for 3 former officers in Tyre Nichols' death
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Moments after a federal judge read the partial convictions in the federal trial of three former Memphis officers in the fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, an activist outside the courtroom could not contain his shock. “What the (expletive)?” Hunter Demster exclaimed to a reporter.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China could wage economic war on Taiwan to force surrender, report says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. military officials and analysts have for years warned of possible armed attacks or blockades by China on Taiwan, but a report released Friday has raised a red flag about possible non-military tactics that could be used effectively against the self-governed island.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: U.S. adds more jobs, unemployment dips to 4.1%
item.description: BAKERSFIELD, Calif. (KGET) -- The U.S. added 254,000 jobs in September, while the unemployment rate fell from 4.2% in August to 4.1%. The unemployment rate was expected to be unchanged from August.
item.dc:creator: Luis Garcia, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Several bands drop out of Orlando heavy metal festival after organizers book Kyle Rittenhouse as guest
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. — An upcoming heavy metal festival set to take place in Florida this month is undergoing some major lineup changes after several bands that were set to perform at the event dropped out after learning that Kyle Rittenhouse had been invited to participate.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, WGN 9

item.title: Arrests made in Memphis kidnapping and violent robbery
item.description: A man told police he was kidnapped at gunpoint from the Fair View Inn on Sycamore View Road by two men and two women.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Moon, NewsNation

item.title: Satellite shows before-and-after flooding in North Carolina after Helene
item.description: Planet Labs captured and compared satellite images that show the storm's devastating impact on one of the hardest hit cities.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Ullmann, Fox 5

item.title: Oklahoma defends Bibles-in-schools proposal after report that only Trump's might qualify
item.description: Oklahoma Education Superintendent Ryan Walters’s (R) office is defending the process for how the state will be selecting newly mandated Bibles in classrooms after a report was released that the criteria is so narrow that essentially no Bibles qualify — besides ones endorsed by former President Trump
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KRON4

item.title: US launches airstrikes on Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels
item.description: Military aircraft and warships bombed Houthi strongholds at roughly five locations, according to the officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Tennessee agencies warn against Helene recovery scams
item.description: Several state and federal agencies have issued warnings about Helene recovery scams and have given advice on how to avoid becoming a victim yourself.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Barker, NewsNation

item.title: Giant bear claws out from under Southern California home
item.description: Bear break-ins in the San Gabriel foothill communities are common and most homeowners have learned to cope with the big, furry nuisances.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, NewsNation

item.title: What is Marburg virus? Outbreak of Ebola-like disease detected in yet another country
item.description: Health officials in Rwanda are dealing with the country’s first outbreak of the Marburg virus, an Ebola-like disease which, if left untreated, has a fatality rate of up to 88%.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk to attend Trump rally at site of attempted assassination
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said he plans to attend former President Donald Trump’s rally in battleground Pennsylvania this weekend when Trump returns to the site where he survived the first assassination attempt on his life.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Nigerian track star Oba Femi shines as NXT superstar
item.description: Femi will defend his title Tuesday night on the CW Network. It's a show that also features WWE legend Randy Orton.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, Fox 5

item.title: Rosie O’Donnell: Knowing the Menendez brothers has been ‘healing’
item.description: "Back in 1989, no one was ready to believe that fathers raped their sons. But they do,” O’Donnell told NewsNation’s “CUOMO.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: When will the power return? Weary Carolinas residents long for relief after Helene's fury
item.description: Nearly 700,000 homes and businesses in the U.S. Southeast are still without power more than a week after Hurricane Helene blew through the region.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Amy, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Two human cases of bird flu confirmed in California as infection continues to spread in US: CDC
item.description: The Centers of Disease Control confirmed on Thursday that two additional cases of the bird flu were identified in California, upping the number to 16 cases in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mixed verdict for 3 Memphis officers convicted in Tyre Nichols' fatal beating
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. (AP) — Three former Memphis police officers were convicted Thursday in the 2023 fatal beating of Tyre Nichols, but were acquitted of the harshest charges they faced for a death that sparked national protests and calls for broad changes in policing.
item.dc:creator: ADRIAN SAINZ, KRISTIN M. HALL and JONATHAN MATTISE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Springsteen calls Trump 'most dangerous candidate for president in my lifetime,' backs Harris
item.description: The rock legend said the former president “doesn’t understand the meaning of this country, its history or what it means to be deeply American.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: North Carolina election officials grapple with storm's impact
item.description: Reporter Bob Buckley from NewsNation affiliate WGHP says the impact of Helene in North Carolina could have "massive" impact on the election.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Twin babies who died alongside their mother are youngest-known Helene victims
item.description: A large tree had crashed through the roof, crushing Kobe Williams and causing her to fall on top of infant sons Khyzier and Khazmir. All three were found dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Rhode Island woman works 350 hours to build first home
item.description: "It makes you excited to think that you're going to build your own home and help others to get theirs," she told NewsNatoin affiliate WPRI. "That's like a dream come true.
item.dc:creator: Catie McNeil, NewsNation

item.title: Dockworkers' union to suspend strike until Jan. 15
item.description: The union representing 45,000 striking U.S. dockworkers at East and Gulf coast ports reached a deal Thursday to suspend a three-day strike until Jan. 15 to provide time to negotiate a new contract.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Los Angeles prosecutors to review new evidence in Menendez brothers' 1996 murder conviction
item.description: Prosecutors in Los Angeles say they are reviewing new evidence in the case of the Menendez brothers to determine whether they should be serving life sentences for killing their parents in their Beverly Hills mansion more than 35 years ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Country music star Garth Brooks accused of sexual assault by makeup artist
item.description: Garth Brooks, the country music superstar who gained fame in the 1990s with several hit songs, has been named in a lawsuit accusing him of raping a hair and makeup artist in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, Fox 5

item.title: Los Angeles D.A. to ‘reevaluate’ Menendez brothers’ convictions
item.description: Los Angeles County District Attorney George Gascón provided an update Thursday on the convictions of Erik and Lyle Menendez, who were convicted of killing their parents in August 1989.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Federal agencies warn of scams in wake of Helene
item.description: “These criminals take advantage of victims before, during, and after a natural disaster strikes, targeting people when they are most vulnerable, and their methods to commit fraud are wide-ranging,” one U.S. Attorney said.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KRON4

item.title: How you can help after Helene
item.description: Reporters who rode out the storm will explain what Americans faced in the hours after Helene arrived. Red Cross representatives will also explain how your donations are being used to help Americans across 10 states get back on their feet.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, Fox 5

item.title: Chances increase for tropical development in the Gulf
item.description: Hurricane Kirk is expected to strengthen bringing swells to the U.S. east coast on Sunday, the National Hurricane Center said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Grandparents found hugging one another after fallen tree killed them in their South Carolina home
item.description: As Hurricane Helene roared outside, the wind howling and branches snapping, John Savage went to his grandparents' bedroom to make sure they were OK.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Hurricane Helene renews calls for Congress to pass disaster funding
item.description: The widespread devastation wrought by Hurricane Helene is upping the pressure on lawmakers for a looming end-of-year fight over disaster funding.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Iran fires at least 180 missiles into Israel as regionwide conflict grows
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, the latest in a series of escalating attacks in a yearslong conflict between Israel and Iran and its Arab allies that threatens to push the Middle East closer toward a regionwide war.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani, Fox 5
